title: xdotool type gets intercepted by an active IME and the target receives converted gibberish
summary: Agent types ASCII into a GUI field via xdotool type on a machine with a CJK input method enabled; the IME intercepts the synthetic key events and converts them, so the field receives transformed characters instead of the literal string.
mistake: To fill Chrome's tab-search box the agent ran xdotool type "navi.onamae" on a desktop with ibus (Japanese IME) active. The IME intercepted the synthetic keystrokes and composed them into kana, so the search box received garbled text and matched nothing. This bites even for pure-ASCII strings: whether the text survives depends on the focused field's IME state, which the agent cannot reliably know.
instead: Never send literal strings to GUI fields with xdotool type when an IME may be active. Put the string on the clipboard and paste instead: printf '%s' 'the-string' | xclip -selection clipboard, then xdotool key --clearmodifiers ctrl+v. Paste bypasses the IME composition layer entirely. Use the same pattern for URL entry (ctrl+l then ctrl+v). Reserve xdotool key for chords (ctrl+..., Return, Escape), which pass through IMEs safely.
